name: Sonix
description: Automated multilingual transcription with editing and export

first-release.comment: Company/product founded 2017; only the founding year is disclosed, exact launch month not stated, so recorded at year precision.

status.comment: Actively developed and sold as of June 2026.

license.comment: Proprietary SaaS. Borderline freemium vs proprietary: there is no ongoing free plan, only a 30-minute free trial, but it functions as a freemium-style entry. See free-tier note.

primary-type.comment: Core job is automated transcription and translation of uploaded audio/video files with a browser-based editor. Not a speak-to-type dictation tool and not a meeting-bot assistant.

real-time-dictation.comment: Sonix is a batch transcriber (upload/record then transcribe); it is not a live speak-to-type input method.

file-transcription.comment: Core feature: upload audio/video files (MP3, WAV, M4A, FLAC, MP4, MOV, etc.) for transcription.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No live meeting bot. Zoom integration transcribes cloud recordings after the meeting ends; Sonix Recorder records audio and transcribes only after you stop. This is post-recording file transcription, not live capture.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Batch processing (~<4 min per hour of audio); no running real-time transcript of ongoing audio.

audio-sources.comment: File upload; in-app/mobile recording via Sonix Recorder (microphone); and URL/web import (e.g. YouTube). No live meeting bot.

underlying-engine.comment: Sonix markets its own AI transcription engine; it does not disclose wrapping a third-party model such as Whisper. Treated as proprietary.

language-count.comment: Approx. Vendor states 54+ languages for transcription; translation covers 55+ languages. Counts shift with releases.

english-accuracy.comment: Independent reviews report ~95-99% on clean, clearly-spoken English (vendor claims 99%), but accuracy drops noticeably on accents, overlapping speakers and conversational speech, sometimes needing substantial manual correction. No published WER from a controlled benchmark found; rated 4 on strong clean-audio performance with the accent/cross-talk caveat.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Supports many languages but the language is selected manually at upload; no documented automatic language detection (review notes accent selection is English-only).

custom-vocabulary.comment: Custom Dictionary feature lets users add company names, technical terms and jargon to boost recognition.

timestamps.comment: Time-aligned transcripts with word/segment timestamps synced to playback.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Generates AI summaries, chapters and sentiment analysis from transcripts.

built-in-editor.comment: Browser-based editor that lets you 'edit audio by editing text' (media-by-text style), with notes, version history and find & replace.

translation.comment: Translates transcripts into 55+ languages with side-by-side comparison and multi-language subtitle export.

export-formats.comment: Word (.docx), TXT, PDF, SRT and VTT documented; also offers FCPXML for video editors. JSON is available via the API rather than as a listed export format.

processing-location.comment: Cloud-only SaaS; audio is uploaded and processed on Sonix servers (TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest). No on-device option.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud service; requires a network connection to upload and transcribe.

data-used-for-training.comment: Sonix states uploaded customer data (audio, transcripts, files) is never used to train its models; 'nothing to opt out of.' Checked June 2026.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can view, export and delete media/transcripts at any time and set automatic-deletion/retention policies.

platforms.comment: Browser-based web app. Sonix Recorder is an installable PWA that adds to the home screen on iOS and Android (same login), but there is no native macOS/Windows desktop app; mobile use is via the web/PWA rather than dedicated App Store/Play Store apps.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; transcription happens within Sonix, not injected OS-wide into other apps.

integrations.comment: Integrates with Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Google Meet, Webex (post-recording transcription), plus Zapier and Adobe Premiere. Webex/Zapier/Premiere have no dedicated tag in the schema.

api-automation.comment: Offers a RESTful API for automated transcription and batch processing.

pricing-model.comment: Monthly/annual subscription tiers (Core/Advanced/Pro) plus a usage-based Pay-As-You-Go option at $10/hr. Checked June 2026.

free-tier.comment: Only a 30-minute free trial (no credit card required), not an ongoing free plan.

free-allowance.comment: No ongoing free tier; 30 minutes is a one-time trial, not a recurring allowance.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid option is Pay-As-You-Go at $10/hr with no monthly commitment; cheapest fixed monthly plan is Core at $25/mo (5 hrs, ~$22.92/mo billed annually). Recorded the $10 PAYG entry point. Checked June 2026.
